explicit_text: I was born in Burma, in a jungle beside a river, during a very difficult time. The war between Burmese soldiers and Karen soldiers was ongoing when I was born, and my family had to flee into the forest for safety. The village we lived in had already been burned by soldiers, so it was not safe to stay there. Burma is where my early life struggles began, surrounded by conflict and hardship.

The village we lived in had already been burned by soldiers, so it was not safe to stay there. This country is where my early life struggles began, surrounded by conflict and hardship.
cues: war between Burmese and Karen soldiers

explicit_text: I belong to the Karen people, who have been caught in conflict with Burmese soldiers for many years. My family and I experienced the violence firsthand, especially during the war that was happening when I was born. The Karen people have had to flee their homes multiple times, including moving to refugee camps in Thailand. Our identity as Karen has shaped much of my early life and the struggles my family faced.
implicit_text: I belong to an ethnic group that has been caught in conflict with the national army of my country for many years. My family and I experienced the violence firsthand, especially during the war that was happening when I was born. Members of this group have had to flee their homes multiple times, including moving to refugee camps in a neighboring Southeast Asian country. Our identity as part of this ethnic group has shaped much of my early life and the struggles my family faced.

explicit_text: After being in the forest for a while, my family moved to Karen Htee Ma Ta village, which is located in Karen State. This was around the time I was a few months old, and my mom worked hard there to support us. Karen State was supposed to be our home, but the war restarted in 1998, forcing us to leave again. It was a place tied to both hope and hardship for my family.

It was a place tied to both hope and hardship for my family.

explicit_text: Karen Htee Ma Ta is the village in Karen State where my family settled after escaping the forest. My mom had to work very hard there to feed us, especially after the difficult birth and our time in the jungle. Unfortunately, the war started again when I was just over a year old, and we had to leave Karen Htee Ma Ta to find safety elsewhere. That village represents a brief period of relative stability for my family during a turbulent time.

explicit_text: When the war restarted in 1998, my family had to flee to a refugee camp in Thailand. The journey was long and difficult, taking about a month on foot, and my mom carried me on her back the entire time. Thailand became a place of refuge after escaping the violence in Karen State. It was there that I first experienced a small sense of safety, even though life was still very hard.

explicit_text: Now, my family and I live in the United States of America, which feels like heaven compared to where we came from. Here, I no longer have to run from soldiers or hear gunshots, and I believe that if I work hard, my future will be bright. America represents hope, education, and success for me, a place where my goals can finally be reached. It is the sweet part of my story after so much bitterness.
